03.06|name.rationale: Dyn 3 brace bracket on book p187 (scan-105-right). Agent C dropped this row entirely; agents A and B split 1/1 on `name` (A=`Ahu (Huni, Aches)` keeping the compound parenthetical inline, B=bare `Ahu`). Per fix_rows.py discriminator: when the compound parenthetical with internal commas is two name-form variants of a single concept (here `Huni, Aches` are Egyptian + Greek nomen variants), the compound stays inline in `name` with `egyptian_titulary` null. Agent A's value is the canonical form; cross-row references in the Dyn 3 brace-bracket cohort (`03.04` editorial_notes references `Ahu (Huni, Aches) (03.06)`) require this exact spelling.

03.06|egyptian_titulary.rationale: Dyn 3 brace bracket on book p187 (scan-105-right). Agent C dropped this row; agents A and B split 1/1 on titulary (A=null, B=`Huni, Aches`). Per the compound-stays-in-name discriminator (see `03.06|name` above), the compound parenthetical lives in `name`, not `egyptian_titulary` — agent A's null is correct. Agent B's extraction wrongly split a compound that should stay inline.

03.06|egyptian_titulary_kind.rationale: Dyn 3 brace bracket on book p187 (scan-105-right). Follows from `03.06|egyptian_titulary` being null — kind is null when titulary is null per README schema (`egyptian_titulary_kind` ∈ {`null`, ...} `null when egyptian_titulary is null`). Agent A=null, B=`nomen`; A is correct because the compound parenthetical stays in `name` rather than splitting into titulary+kind.

Te-wosret` (space, no parens), C=`Kgin.Te-wosret` (no space, no parens). Resolution combines two rules: (1) the Co-regent queen rule (prompt.md) splits Te-wosret into a separate row with `name="Kgin.<queen-name>"` and the parenthetical `(Thuoris)` extracted into `egyptian_titulary`; (2) the Kgin spacing standardisation (verified across all 5 queen rows on scan-106-right and scan-107-left) prints `Kgin.` with NO space before the queen's name. Agent C's `Kgin.Te-wosret` matches both rules verbatim.
